postData.contentHtml:
A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck the southern Mindanao island in the Philippines on June 8, resulting in a tragic toll of at least 61 lives lost and 40 people reported missing. Beyond the immediate devastation, the tremor unleashed a remarkable geological phenomenon: coastal uplift.

Triggered by a shifting of the Cotabato Trench, the earthquake caused the seabed to rise by as much as 2 meters in certain areas. This dramatic uplift led to the extension of shorelines by up to 200 meters along the coastlines of Sarangani and Davao Occidental provinces, exposing vast stretches of what was once submerged marine environment.

The environmental repercussions have been significant. Long stretches of coral reefs and seagrass beds, suddenly exposed to the air, have begun to die off. This has, in turn, led to the demise of resident marine organisms, including reef fish, eels, clams, and shells. Local residents have expressed concerns about the potential for environmental contamination and unpleasant fumes from the decaying marine life.

The Cotabato Trench is known for its frequent seismic activity, having experienced a 'swarm' of thousands of smaller earthquakes earlier in January. This latest event underscores the dynamic geological nature of the region and the profound impact such natural forces can have on both human communities and the delicate balance of coastal ecosystems.

Social media sentiment surrounding the earthquake, coastal uplift, and shoreline extension has been largely positive, though some debate exists, reflecting ongoing discussions about the long-term environmental and recovery efforts.
postData.title: Philippines Earthquake: How a Powerful Quake Extended Shorelines and Impacted Marine Life
postData.summary: A recent 7.8-magnitude earthquake in the southern Philippines not only caused tragic loss of life but also dramatically altered the landscape, raising the seabed by up to 2 meters and extending shorelines by hundreds of meters, with significant consequences for marine ecosystems.

sources.title: How Philippines quake extended shorelines for hundreds of metres
Deadly Philippine quake raised seabed by up to 2m
